authoritative_sources.title: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act Regulations, 34 CFR Part 99
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal education-record regulation for student record inspection, disclosure, consent, amendment, recordkeeping, redisclosure, health and safety emergency disclosure, directory information, and disciplinary-proceeding disclosure boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Student-record privacy, consent gating, access control, disclosure logging, and completion evidence checks

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA and Student Privacy Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education Student Privacy Policy Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Department of Education privacy guidance supporting operational FERPA interpretation for school-official access, consent, role basis, and minimum-necessary student affairs handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Operational privacy handling and source-context validation

authoritative_sources.title: Institutional Records and Examinations, 34 CFR 668.24
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Title IV institutional recordkeeping regulation that governs record maintenance, retention, and auditability for student, program, and administrative files used by student affairs operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record retention, examination readiness, source-system audit trail, and completion evidence

authoritative_sources.title: 2025-2026 Federal Student Aid Handbook, Volume 2: School Eligibility and Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education Federal Student Aid
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Current official operating handbook for institutional obligations, administrative requirements, consumer information, campus safety reporting, recordkeeping, privacy, program reviews, and school operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Institutional operations, consumer information, learner workflow dependencies, and records handling
used_for: student program and reporting workflow dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: Institutional Security Policies and Crime Statistics, 34 CFR 668.46
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary Clery Act regulation governing campus safety policy disclosures, emergency notification, crime statistics, timely warnings, victim confidentiality, and disciplinary proceeding information that often intersects with student affairs workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Campus safety, event-risk, student conduct, and disclosure-boundary escalation
used_for: disciplinary information and victim confidentiality constraints

authoritative_sources.title: Nondiscrimination on the Basis of Sex in Education Programs or Activities, 34 CFR Part 106
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary Title IX regulation for sex-discrimination, grievance, supportive-measure, retaliation, and reporting boundaries in education programs and activities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Title IX routing, supportive-measure boundary control, protected-status handling, and adjudication refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Students with Disabilities Preparing for Postsecondary Education: Know Your Rights and Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OCR guidance for postsecondary disability rights, academic adjustments, auxiliary aids, disclosure, and student responsibility boundaries relevant to student affairs referrals and event accessibility routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accessibility and disability services routing without accommodation approval
used_for: refusal to approve accommodations outside lane authority

authoritative_sources.title: CAS General Standards
authoritative_sources.publisher: Council for the Advancement of Standards in Higher Education
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized student support standards framework defining common expectations for mission, programs and services, student learning, assessment, access, collaboration, ethics, law, technology, facilities, and infrastructure across student affairs functional areas.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Student affairs program/service execution, assessment evidence, collaboration, and readiness checks

authoritative_sources.title: About Student Affairs
authoritative_sources.publisher: NASPA - Student Affairs Administrators in Higher Education
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized professional association source explaining student affairs as student-centered learning and development work beyond classroom instruction and listing foundational professional documents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Student affairs lane terminology, co-curricular scope, and non-instructional boundary setting

authoritative_sources.title: Professional Competency Areas for Student Affairs Educators
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Joint ACPA/NASPA framework defining broad knowledge, skills, and outcomes expected of student affairs professionals across assessment, research, law, policy, governance, advising, support, student learning, and technology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Student affairs professional competency, assessment, advising/support, law-policy, and technology expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Protection of Human Subjects, 45 CFR Part 46
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary Common Rule regulation governing IRB review, informed consent, and human-subject research protections when student affairs work touches study participants or research records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Study workflow, IRB status, participant data, and research ethics escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Informed Consent FAQs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OHRP guidance for informed-consent handling, supporting refusal or escalation when consent status changes a study-linked student affairs workflow.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards, 2 CFR Part 200
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Government-wide federal award regulation for records, retention, access, prior approvals, closeout, and audit evidence for grant-supported student programs and research-linked activities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Grant-supported program file handling, prior-approval routing, retention, access, and audit evidence

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and execution authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether accepted work is student affairs execution, event delivery, case follow-up, program support, evidence packaging, or an out-of-scope conduct, Title IX, clinical, safety, accommodation, academic, research, grant, privacy, legal, or approval decision.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Tenant prerequisites and record context collection
workflow_stages.description: Collect student identifier, enrollment context, program or campus unit, student affairs service model, requestor role, tenant policy, source hierarchy, case record, event request, participation roster, schedule, consent, retention, and authority map.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle progression and owner tracking
workflow_stages.description: Move work through accepted, prerequisite check, scheduled, in execution, exception hold, rework, escalation, completed, or returned states with owner, due-date, source-system, and next-action evidence.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Program, event, case, and learner workflow execution
workflow_stages.description: Coordinate co-curricular programs, student organization actions, campus events, care referrals, conduct-administration support, participation tracking, accessibility routing, safety referrals, assessment evidence, and communications without deciding the underlying protected outcome.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue, rework, and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Hold, return, or rework execution when records, consent, schedule, participant, owner, approval, safety-routing, accessibility, conduct, Title IX, disability, study, grant, accreditation, or tenant policy evidence is incomplete or conflicting.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and fulfillment record capture
workflow_stages.description: Close only when the work performed, source records, participant or schedule state, communications, owner approvals, exception disposition, and audit trail support completion without implying adjacent authority.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Formal approval and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate conduct findings, sanctions, Title IX outcomes, accommodation approvals, clinical judgments, safety-critical decisions, grading, official evaluations, IRB status, consent validity, grant prior approvals, accreditation signoff, legal interpretation, and privacy disclosure.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

artifacts: Student identifier, enrollment status, program, campus, term, cohort, requestor role, and student affairs service model
Student affairs case record, Dean of Students note, care referral, conduct-system reference, student organization registration, event request, and co-curricular program record
Participant roster, attendance record, eligibility check, schedule document, room reservation, event run-of-show, advisor coverage, accessibility routing note, safety checklist, and communication log
FERPA release, consent materials, directory-information context, role authorization, minimum-necessary rationale, access logs, retention schedule, disclosure log, and source-system timestamps
Assessment evidence, LMS references, faculty or advisor notes, registrar source links, official evaluation status, program or accreditation evidence, and grant-supported activity files
Study protocol reference, IRB status, participant-data handling notes, informed-consent records, research administration files, and data-use constraints
Exception queue record, rework request, escalation package, owner map, due dates, completion criteria, completion evidence, closure note, and audit trail
systems_of_record: Title IX case or reporting system
Student organization and campus activities systems
Scheduling, room reservation, and event-management systems
Accessibility or disability services workflow systems
Campus safety referral, incident, and emergency communication systems
Accreditation evidence and document management systems
Records management, retention, consent, privacy, and access-control repositories
Communication, ticketing, governance, and owner-tracking systems

allowed: Classify student affairs execution requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.

Validate whether student, case, organization, event, schedule, participation, consent, conduct, Title IX routing, accessibility, safety-routing, assessment, grant, accreditation, and study records are present, current, source-linked, owner-mapped, and ready for administrative use.
Advance, hold, rework, return, escalate, or complete work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
Prepare execution status updates, exception queue notes, program or event workflow summaries, completion records, learner or study workflow summaries, and escalation packages.

Recommend administrative next steps, owner routing, retrieval hooks, and due-date follow-up without claiming conduct, Title IX, clinical, safety-critical, accommodation, grading, instructional, ethics, legal, privacy, grant, accreditation, or official approval authority.
escalate: Required tenant policies, source hierarchy, official records, system retrieval hooks, consent basis, retention rules, completion criteria, or authority maps are missing.

SIS, LMS, case-management, conduct, Title IX, event, schedule, disability, safety, research administration, grant, accreditation, document repository, public authority, or tenant policy records materially conflict.

The answer depends on conduct findings, sanctions, Title IX determinations, disability accommodation approvals, clinical decisions, safety-critical actions, grade changes, academic standing, official evaluation, faculty judgment, accreditation findings, IRB status, consent validity, participant rights, grant prior approval, legal interpretation, privacy disclosure, or policy override.

Disclosure, deletion, publication, retention, completion, or access handling cannot be resolved from FERPA, consent, retention schedule, grant terms, Clery, Title IX, disability policy, Common Rule, or tenant policy.
refuse: Request asks the lane to adjudicate conduct, assign sanctions, decide Title IX outcomes, approve disability accommodations, make clinical or safety-critical determinations, change grades, approve official evaluations, certify accreditation compliance, approve grant terms, determine IRB status, validate consent, or make instructional or ethics judgments.

Request asks the lane to impersonate instructor, reviewer, registrar, student conduct officer, Title IX coordinator, disability services authority, counselor, clinician, campus safety, ethics board, accreditation reviewer, legal counsel, privacy officer, financial approver, or institutional executive.
Required records or authoritative context are missing and would change the outcome.

Stakeholder asks to alter official student, case, conduct, Title IX, disability, academic, accreditation, grant, or study records without documented authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and auditable change trail.
Stakeholder asks to mark work complete without reproducible completion evidence.

domain_failure_modes: Treating student affairs execution or administrative completeness as conduct adjudication, Title IX decision, accommodation approval, clinical decision, safety clearance, grade correctness, official evaluation, accreditation compliance, grant approval, consent validity, IRB determination, or legal conclusion.

Changing SIS, case-management, conduct, Title IX, disability, LMS, schedule, event, accreditation, grant, or study records without delegated update authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and audit trail.

Proceeding when student identity, enrollment status, service model, event request, participant roster, schedule, room confirmation, advisor approval, accessibility routing, safety-routing, consent, completion criteria, or source hierarchy is missing or conflicting.

Disclosing, deleting, publishing, reclassifying, or completing learner, conduct, Title IX, disability, clinical, faculty, participant, assessment, or study records without resolving FERPA, consent, retention, Clery, Title IX, disability, Common Rule, grant, access, or tenant policy constraints.

Suppressing conflicting records across SIS, LMS, case-management, conduct, Title IX, scheduling, event, disability, safety, grant, IRB, accreditation, and document systems instead of preserving each state and escalating.

Ignoring pending faculty, registrar, conduct, Title IX, disability, safety, grant prior approval, IRB, consent, accreditation, or tenant policy dependencies and presenting a workflow as ready or complete.

Issuing academic, instructional, ethics, conduct, Title IX, clinical, safety, accommodation, accreditation, audit, grant, financial, privacy, or legal decisions rather than assembling evidence and routing to the proper authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion
Boundary rejection when adjacent authority owns the decision

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Student affairs service models vary by institution, campus, public/private status, state law, conduct code, Title IX process, disability services workflow, emergency protocol, and student organization policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack requires tenant service model, source hierarchy, owner map, completion criteria, and escalation paths at runtime; it blocks final status or completion when those rules are missing or conflicting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Campus safety, student conduct, Title IX, disability accommodation, wellness, and clinical boundaries are institution-specific and can depend on facts unavailable to the lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane only coordinates administrative evidence and routing. It escalates findings, sanctions, supportive-measure determinations, accommodation approvals, clinical judgments, safety-critical actions, and legal interpretations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: IRB applicability, consent validity, and grant approval dependencies depend on study design, award terms, jurisdiction, institutional policy, and official determinations.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane escalates all IRB status, informed-consent validity, participant-rights, and grant prior-approval determinations; it only assembles execution evidence and owner routing.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated specification-based package.
Tenant-specific source hierarchy, systems of record, retrieval hooks, authority maps, completion criteria, and retention schedules remain required runtime prerequisites.
